line_item_fields.name: Job Name (or "Lamex PO" on some receipts)

validation_rules: If totals exist: total ~= subtotal + tax
If line items exist: total ~= sum(line_items.amount)
instructions:
Extraction Instructions:

1. Vendor:  
   - Extract from the sender name or the first distinct name at the document top above major headers.  
   - If ambiguous, use the value nearest "From:" or as the sender in email format.

2. Invoice/Receipt Number:  
   - Anchor on "Order #", "Invoice #", "Receipt #", "Order Number", "Invoice No", "Bill No".  
   - Extract the numeric or alphanumeric identifier following these.  
   - If absent, return NaN.

3. Transaction/Invoice Date:  
   - Anchor on "Date", "Order Date", "Invoice Date", "Sent", or standard date patterns (MM/DD/YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, Month DD, YYYY).  
   - Select the date nearest label, normalizing to YYYY-MM-DD.  
   - If absent, return NaN.

4. Invoice/Receipt Subtotal:  
   - Anchor on "Subtotal", "Sub-total", or "Amount before Tax".  
   - Extract numeric value, removing all commas, currency symbols, or extraneous characters.  
   - Normalize as plain decimal (e.g., 2902.64).

5. Tax:  
   - Anchor on "Tax", "Sales Tax", "GST", or similar.  
   - Extract the adjacent numeric value.  
   - Remove commas or currency characters and normalize.  
   - If not present, return 0.

6. Invoice/Receive Total:  
   - Anchor on "Grand Total", "Total", "Amount Due", "Total Amount", or "Invoice/Receipt Total".  
   - Prefer value that is after "Subtotal"/"Tax" and closest to page bottom.  
   - Remove commas/currency characters and normalize.

7. Service Location:  
   - Anchor on "Service Location", "Job Site", or "Work Location".  
   - If not present, return NaN.

8. Shipping Address:  
   - Anchor on "Shipping Address", "Ship To".  
   - Extract lines starting beneath/next to label up to an empty line or next major label.  
   - Concatenate as a single string, replacing line breaks with commas.

9. Billing Address:  
   - Anchor on "Billing Address", "Bill To".  
   - Extract lines beneath/next to label up to an empty line or major label.  
   - Concatenate as a single string, replacing line breaks with commas.

10. Handwritten Note(s):  
    - Detect any text not printed (scanned handwriting or markup).  
    - If none, return NaN.

11. Document Name:  
    - Use provided filename if available, otherwise from header/footer or as linked to Bates number.

12. Page Number:  
    - Extract from explicit "Page X", numerals in footer/header, or deduced from sequence.  
    - Use integer, not padded.

13. Bates Number:  
    - Anchor on any alphanumeric code matching document IDs, typically in header/footer or filename, that reflects pagination or unique ID (e.g., CDII_003366-67).  
    - Use as single string.

Line Item Table Instructions:

- Table region:  
  - Detect by locating a section beneath order details with product or service descriptions, quantities, and amounts.  
  - Use lines where columns parse as "Description", "Item", "Qty", "Amount", "Total", "PO" or similar.

- Line mapping:  
  - Item Description:  
    - Text from the product/description column in each row.  
    - If multiple fields, concatenate as needed.
  - Item Amount:  
    - Amount in same row as description, normalize numbers (no commas, currency symbols).
  - Job Name ("Lamex PO"):  
    - Anchor on column or pattern "Job", "PO", "Lamex PO" or similar; otherwise, return NaN.

Normalization Rules:  
- For all monetary fields, remove commas and all currency symbols (e.g., "$2,902.64 USD" → 2902.64).  
- Return NaN for missing/empty extracted values (not blank or NULL).  
- Normalize all dates to YYYY-MM-DD.  
- Addresses: flatten to a single comma-separated string with no line breaks.
output_columns: Job Name (or "Lamex PO" on some receipts)
